Recherche

Macron desperately seeks a majority in the Assemblée Nationale                The French President is running out of time in the scrabble for seats in the legislative elections. After a lackluster first round, Emmanual Macron is continuing his campaign of silence in the apparent hope that apathy will win his party enough votes.

Subscribers onlyFemicides draw attention in Turkey as government 'fails to protect victims'  Although a fairly sophisticated legal arsenal exists in Turkish law to combat femicides,  hundreds of Turkish women die every year at the hands of their husbands and partners.

Subscribers only'A Stanford MBA is a €220,000 social ladder'  This week, 29-year-old Thomas Sengmany from Paris talks about his experience as an Asian student among the social and intellectual elite at Stanford University in the United States.

Subscribers onlyElton John has saved the best for last  The British star performed a medley of hits from the 1970s for the first Paris dates of his farewell tour at the Paris La Défense Arena on Saturday and Sunday, June 11-12.

Investigation   Lucas Minisini Subscribers onlyThe puzzle of Havana syndrome, the strange disease affecting North American spies and diplomats Headaches, dizziness, severe fatigue: Since 2016, mysterious neurological disorders, dubbed 'Havana syndrome,' have been afflicting Canadian and American diplomats and members of the secret services. Despite a lack of public evidence, Ottawa and Washington strongly suspect that Russia is behind it, using a microwave weapon.   13 min read

In Depth   Florence Miettaux Subscribers onlySouth Sudan hit by rural conflicts: 'As herds grow bigger, tension increases' Clashes between farmers and herders have left dozens dead since the beginning of the year, mainly in the Central Equatoria region.   7 min read
